## Shared contract (frozen ‚Äî every surface MUST conform to this)

These decisions are fixed for the whole epic so the independently-built surfaces compose. Do not
invent your own variants.

### Shared data shapes
- **Project membership lives in `ctx.db.members`** ‚Äî an array of records `{ projectId, userId, role }`. One record per (project, user). To add a membership, push such a record (replacing any existing record for the same project+user). To test whether a user is a member, look for a matching record in `ctx.db.members`. If it is undefined, treat it as an empty array and create it.
- **Vault membership lives in `ctx.db.vaultMembers`** ‚Äî an array of records `{ vaultId, userId, role }`. One record per (vault, user). To add a membership, push such a record (replacing any existing record for the same vault+user). To test whether a user is a member, look for a matching record in `ctx.db.vaultMembers`. If it is undefined, treat it as an empty array and create it.
- **Tracker membership lives in `ctx.db.trackerMembers`** ‚Äî an array of records `{ trackerId, userId, role }`. One record per (tracker, user). To add a membership, push such a record (replacing any existing record for the same tracker+user). To test whether a user is a member, look for a matching record in `ctx.db.trackerMembers`. If it is undefined, treat it as an empty array and create it.
- **Channel membership lives in `ctx.db.channelMembers`** ‚Äî an array of records `{ channelId, userId, role }`. One record per (channel, user). To add a membership, push such a record (replacing any existing record for the same channel+user). To test whether a user is a member, look for a matching record in `ctx.db.channelMembers`. If it is undefined, treat it as an empty array and create it.
- **Board membership lives in `ctx.db.boardMembers`** ‚Äî an array of records `{ boardId, userId, role }`. One record per (board, user). To add a membership, push such a record (replacing any existing record for the same board+user). To test whether a user is a member, look for a matching record in `ctx.db.boardMembers`. If it is undefined, treat it as an empty array and create it.
- project records are `{ id, orgId, name }` in `ctx.db.projects`; vault records are `{ id, orgId, name }` in `ctx.db.vaults`; tracker records are `{ id, orgId, name }` in `ctx.db.trackers`; channel records are `{ id, orgId, name }` in `ctx.db.channels`; board records are `{ id, orgId, name }` in `ctx.db.boards`.
- comment records are `{ id, projectId, authorId, orgId, body, createdAt }` in `ctx.db.comments`; file records are `{ id, vaultId, authorId, orgId, body, createdAt }` in `ctx.db.files`; ticket records are `{ id, trackerId, authorId, orgId, body, createdAt }` in `ctx.db.tickets`; message records are `{ id, channelId, authorId, orgId, body, createdAt }` in `ctx.db.messages`; card records are `{ id, boardId, authorId, orgId, body, createdAt }` in `ctx.db.cards`.
- New ids are unique non-empty strings.

### Cross-cutting rules (apply on EVERY surface they touch)
- **Tenancy:** a caller may only read or write records belonging to their own `ctx.session.orgId`. New
  records are stamped with the caller's `orgId`, never a client-supplied one. A read (e.g. listing)
  returns only the caller-org records. Operating across orgs throws.
- **Authorization:** every `add‚Ä¶Member` requires the caller to be an `admin`. Every `post‚Ä¶` requires the
  caller to be a member of that container. `updateProfile` requires the caller to be the target user or
  an admin.
- **No mass-assignment:** `updateProfile` may change only `name` and `bio`; it must never let `patch` set
  `id`, `orgId`, or `role`.
